sections.paragraph:
ἑδράζω fut. 3 sg. ἑδράσει Wsd 4:3; 1 aor. ἥδρασα. Pass. aor. ptc. ἑδρασθείσης 3 Km 3:1, inf. ἑδρασθῆναι LXX; perf. ptc. ἡδρασμένος (ἕδρα ‘seat’; Simias Rhod., fgm 8, 1 Diehl2=24, 1 p. 72 HFränkel=Anth. Pal. 15, 24, 1; Dionys. Hal., Comp. Verb. 6; Heliod. 9, 25; Herm. Wr. 5, 4; LXX; Ath. 13, 1) to firmly establish in a certain place, establish, fix, settle; fig. ἑ. ἐπὶ τὸν τοῦ βουλήματος θεμέλιον fix (someth.) upon the foundation of his will 1 Cl 33:3; τὰ γενόμενα ἑ. establish what exists 60:1. Pass. (Callixenus [III b.c.] : 627 Fgm. 1, 37 Jac. [in Athen. 5, 204d]; Dio Chrys. 1, 78 ἡδρασμένος) ἐν ὁμονοίᾳ θεοῦ established in godly harmony IPhil. ins; ἐν ἀγάπῃ ISm 1:1; πίστει in faith 13:2; γνώμη ἡ. ὡς ἐπὶ πέτραν attitude founded as if upon a rock IPol 1:1 (cp. Sir 22:17).—DELG s.v. ἕζομαι B2.

fut. fut. = fut.

  1. sg. = singular

aor. aor. = aorist

ptc. ptc. = participle

inf. inf. = infinitive

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

perf. perf. = perfect

Diehl Diehl , ed., s. AnthLG—List 5

Comp Comp date uncertain—List 5

Heliod Heliod , III? a.d.—List 5

Herm. Wr. Herm. Wr. = Hermetic Writings—List 5

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

someth. someth. = something

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Jac. Jac. = Die Fragmente der griechischen Historiker, ed. FJacoby—Lists 5, 6

Athen Athen , III a.d.—List 5

Dio Chrys Dio Chrys , I–II a.d.—List 5

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

ISm ISm = Ignatius to the Smyrnaeans, I–II a.d.—List 1

IPol IPol = Ignatius to Polycarp—List 1

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 275
